title: Canonical queue compaction is review-gated
decision: Append-only queue updates are candidate state. They must not be treated as canonical until an explicit reviewed compaction updates operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
observed: The canonical queue remains unchanged while append-only continuity update records exist.
inferred: Readers need an explicit state-layer rule to avoid treating candidate updates as already compacted canonical state.
proposed: All future queue compaction should verify the baseline digest, enumerate accepted candidate updates, preserve rejected updates, and commit one reviewed canonical result.
unresolved: No reviewed compaction has yet promoted M-002 into operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
preservation_rule: Never delete or rewrite candidate updates during compaction; preserve them as historical evidence and record which were accepted, rejected, or deferred.
privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only. Raw private chat, health information, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_or_review_route: Falsify this record only with an explicit reviewed repository decision that changes the compaction rule and preserves the prior rule as superseded history.
